edges.description: Impaired peroxisomal transport and beta-oxidation cause VLCFA buildup in blood and tissues.
edges.source: ABCD1-mediated peroxisomal fatty acid transport defect

edges.description: VLCFA excess promotes redox imbalance and oxidative injury that seeds the cerebral inflammatory cascade.
VLCFA-mediated tissue injury contributes to the inflammatory cerebral phenotype.
VLCFA-mediated injury can also produce the adult spinal-cord and peripheral-nerve-predominant adrenomyeloneuropathy phenotype.
VLCFA accumulation also injures adrenal tissue and contributes to primary adrenal insufficiency.
VLCFA accumulation can also impair gonadal endocrine function.
Redox imbalance contributes to astrocytic metabolic failure and inflammatory activation.
Axonal oxidative stress and energy shortage are implicated in the dying-back axonopathy that underlies the AMN branch of X-ALD.
Astrocyte dysfunction contributes to BBB injury and vascular leakage.
Barrier injury promotes innate immune cell recruitment and activation.
Innate immune activation propagates active white-matter destruction.
Inflammatory white-matter injury is expressed clinically as CNS demyelination.
Active cerebral ALD produces symmetric, expanding white matter lesions on brain MRI.
Activated monocyte/macrophage neuroinflammation is reported by increased CSF chitotriosidase activity.
AMN spinal cord tract disease manifests clinically as progressive spastic paraplegia.
AMN spinal cord and autonomic pathway involvement produces bladder and bowel dysfunction.
Adrenocortical dysfunction is expressed clinically as adrenal insufficiency.
Loss of adrenal cortex steroid hormone secretion is measured as decreased serum cortisol.
Primary adrenal insufficiency is accompanied by compensatory elevation of plasma ACTH.
Primary adrenal insufficiency in ALD can present with increased skin pigmentation.
Addison-form ALD can present with weight loss before neurologic disease is apparent.
Addison-form ALD can present with anorexia as part of adrenal insufficiency.
Gonadal dysfunction is expressed clinically as hypogonadism.
Elevated plasma NfL reports cerebral axonal injury and predicts cALD onset, supporting early intervention decisions.
Elevated plasma VLCFA levels report the biochemical accumulation caused by ABCD1-related peroxisomal transport failure.
Low cortisol reports adrenal cortex failure in ALD-related primary adrenal insufficiency.
Elevated ACTH supports a primary adrenal insufficiency pattern downstream of adrenal cortex dysfunction.
Higher CSF chitotriosidase activity reports active neuroinflammation in cerebral ALD.
CSF matrix metalloproteinase elevation reports blood-brain-barrier extracellular-matrix breakdown in active cerebral ALD.
Higher CSF MMP concentrations correlate with radiographic inflammation and clinical neurologic severity.

edges.target: ABCD1-mediated peroxisomal fatty acid transport defect

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: Adrenoleukodystrophy (ALD) is an X-linked disorder caused by a hemizygous mutation of the ABCD1 gene.

evidence.reference_title: ABCD1 / adrenoleukodystrophy (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: ABCD1 | HGNC:61 | adrenoleukodystrophy | MONDO:0018544 | XL | Definitive

nodes.description: Pathogenic ABCD1 variants impair the peroxisomal transporter required to move very long-chain fatty acids into peroxisomes for degradation.
nodes.id: ABCD1-mediated peroxisomal fatty acid transport defect

biological_processes: very long-chain fatty acid metabolic process

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: Adrenoleukodystrophy (ALD) is an X-linked disorder caused by a hemizygous mutation of the ABCD1 gene.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: The disease results from pathogenic mutations in the peroxisomal transporter ABCD1 gene

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: with high plasma ACTH

readouts.interpretation: Elevated ACTH supports a primary adrenal insufficiency pattern downstream of adrenal cortex dysfunction.

nodes.description: Primary adrenal insufficiency is a major endocrine presentation of ALD and may precede neurologic disease.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: Mediated by elevated concentrations of very long chain fatty acids, the disease may manifest as central nervous system demyelination, primary hypoadrenalism, and/or primary hypogonadism.

nodes.description: ALD frequently involves primary adrenal insufficiency due to adrenal cortex dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: Mediated by elevated concentrations of very long chain fatty acids, the disease may manifest as central nervous system demyelination, primary hypoadrenalism, and/or primary hypogonadism.

nodes.description: The adult AMN branch of ALD preferentially affects spinal cord tracts and peripheral/autonomic pathways, producing progressive spastic paraparesis with bladder and bowel dysfunction.

evidence.snippet: AMN is characterized by leg weakness, spasticity, clumsy gait, pain, and bladder and bowel dysfunction

nodes.description: Anorexia can be part of the adrenal-insufficiency presentation of ALD.

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: anorexia, weight loss, and skin pigmentation from 18 years of age.

nodes.description: ABCD1-mutant astrocytes exhibit metabolic stress and pro-inflammatory signaling changes that help drive cerebral disease.

evidence.reference_title: IPSC-Derived Astrocytes to Model Neuroinflammatory and Metabolic Responses in X-linked Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Mitochondrial function analysis by Seahorse extracellular flux identified increased oxygen consumption and extracellular acidification rates in cALD astrocytes, yet the ATP levels were decreased. Molecular signaling identified increased phosphorylation of STAT3 in cALD astrocytes, and higher proinflammatory cytokine and Toll like receptor (TLR) expression.

nodes.description: Urinary urgency, incontinence, and bowel dysfunction are recognized AMN-associated phenotypes attributable to spinal-cord and autonomic involvement.

evidence.snippet: AMN is characterized by leg weakness, spasticity, clumsy gait, pain, and bladder and bowel dysfunction

nodes.description: Matrix metalloproteinase-mediated BBB injury is a distinct early step in cerebral ALD.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebrospinal fluid matrix metalloproteinases are elevated in cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy and correlate with MRI severity and neurologic dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: Neuroimaging in cALD shows inflammatory changes and indicates blood-brain-barrier (BBB) disruption.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebrospinal fluid matrix metalloproteinases are elevated in cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy and correlate with MRI severity and neurologic dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: MMPs were found to be elevated in the CSF of boys with cALD and may mechanistically contribute to the breakdown of the blood-brain-barrier.

nodes.description: Progressive cerebral white matter demyelination is the major neurologic lesion in cerebral ALD.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: This devastating phenotype is characterized by rapidly progressive central nervous demyelination and, if untreated, usually death within years of onset of clinical signs and symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: the score positively correlated with average concomitant CSF chitotriosidase activity

readouts.interpretation: Higher CSF chitotriosidase activity reports active neuroinflammation in cerebral ALD.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebrospinal fluid matrix metalloproteinases are elevated in cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy and correlate with MRI severity and neurologic dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: There were significant elevations of MMP2, MMP9, MMP10, TIMP1, and total protein in the CSF of boys with cALD compared to controls.

readouts.interpretation: CSF matrix metalloproteinase elevation reports blood-brain-barrier extracellular-matrix breakdown in active cerebral ALD.
Higher CSF MMP concentrations correlate with radiographic inflammation and clinical neurologic severity.

nodes.description: Brain MRI can show symmetric, expanding cerebral white matter lesions before overt neurologic symptoms in childhood cerebral ALD.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: Radiographic changes generally precede clinical neurologic disease by several years in childhood cALD and are characterized by symmetric, expanding white matter lesions.

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: Endocrinological examination showed low serum cortisol

readouts.interpretation: Low cortisol reports adrenal cortex failure in ALD-related primary adrenal insufficiency.

nodes.description: Glucocorticoid replacement treats the cortisol-deficient adrenal insufficiency presentation of ALD.

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: glucocorticoid replacement therapy was initiated.

nodes.description: VLCFA-mediated endocrine tissue injury can also impair gonadal function in some affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: Mediated by elevated concentrations of very long chain fatty acids, the disease may manifest as central nervous system demyelination, primary hypoadrenalism, and/or primary hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Early hematopoietic stem cell transplantation is the key disease-modifying intervention for cerebral ALD.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the concept of peroxisomal diseases and efficient diagnostic system in Japan.
evidence.snippet: In the cerebral type of ALD, hematopoietic stem cell transplantation is the only treatment in the early stage, and thus prompt diagnosis will improve the prognosis of affected patients.

nodes.description: Addison-form ALD can present with increased skin pigmentation as part of primary adrenal insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: anorexia, weight loss, and skin pigmentation from 18 years of age.

nodes.description: Primary hypogonadism can occur as part of the broader endocrine phenotype of adrenoleukodystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: Mediated by elevated concentrations of very long chain fatty acids, the disease may manifest as central nervous system demyelination, primary hypoadrenalism, and/or primary hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Childhood cerebral ALD is marked by rapidly progressive inflammatory white matter demyelination.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: This devastating phenotype is characterized by rapidly progressive central nervous demyelination and, if untreated, usually death within years of onset of clinical signs and symptoms.

nodes.description: Autologous CD34+ hematopoietic stem cells transduced with a Lenti-D lentiviral vector carrying ABCD1 cDNA. FDA-approved for early-stage cerebral ALD. In the phase 2-3 ALD-102 / LTF-304 trials with up to 6 years of follow-up, most boys with early cerebral ALD remained free of major functional disabilities; one case of treatment-related myelodysplastic syndrome was reported, identifying insertional oncogenesis as an ongoing risk.

evidence.reference_title: Lentiviral Gene Therapy for Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: At month 24, none of these 29 patients had major functional disabilities; overall survival was 94%.

nodes.description: Activated innate immune cells drive the inflammatory cerebral lesion in cALD.

evidence.reference_title: Chitotriosidase as a biomarker of cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: As cellular infiltration has been observed in C-ALD, including activation of monocytes and macrophages, we evaluated the activity of chitotriosidase in the plasma and spinal fluid of boys with active C-ALD.

nodes.description: Oxidative stress contributes to cellular injury in X-ALD and amplifies the inflammatory cascade that drives the childhood cerebral phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Oxidative Stress and Inflammation in X-Link Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: research showed that VLCFA could induce oxidative stress and inflammation, leading to damage.

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Cell autonomous processes such as oxidative stress and energy shortage in axons as well as non-cell autonomous processes involving axon-glial interactions seem pertinent to the dying-back axonopathy.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated plasma NfL reports cerebral axonal injury and predicts cALD onset, supporting early intervention decisions.

nodes.description: Progressive spastic paraplegia is the dominant adult neurological manifestation in adrenomyeloneuropathy (AMN), reflecting distal axonopathy of the corticospinal tracts and posterior columns.

evidence.snippet: AMN is characterized by leg weakness, spasticity, clumsy gait, pain, and bladder and bowel dysfunction

nodes.description: Defective degradation causes pathologic elevation of VLCFAs in plasma and tissues, especially affecting brain, spinal cord, and adrenal tissue.

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: Very-long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) levels were elevated, and the ABCD1 mutation, p.Gly116Arg, was identified in hemizygous state.

evidence.reference_title: Intensity of MRI Gadolinium Enhancement in Cerebral Adrenoleukodystrophy: A Biomarker for Inflammation and Predictor of Outcome following Transplantation in Higher Risk Patients.
evidence.snippet: Mediated by elevated concentrations of very long chain fatty acids, the disease may manifest as central nervous system demyelination, primary hypoadrenalism, and/or primary hypogonadism.

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: Very-long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) levels were elevated, and the ABCD1 mutation, p.Gly116Arg, was identified in hemizygous state.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated plasma VLCFA levels report the biochemical accumulation caused by ABCD1-related peroxisomal transport failure.

nodes.description: Weight loss can accompany the Addison-only presentation of ALD before neurologic signs are detected.

evidence.reference_title: A 29-year-old patient with adrenoleukodystrophy presenting with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: At first visit, his weight had decreased by 12 kg from 57 kg when he was 15 years old.
